title: Glazed Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A herbal, chewy hangover cure featuring chicken legs glazed with a tangy pickle brine and aromatic spices, served in a cast‑iron skillet.

ingredients.name: bone-in chicken legs
ingredients.notes: season with salt and pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat your cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Drizzle olive oil, then brown the chicken legs on all sides for about 8 minutes.
Remove chicken and set aside; add onion and garlic to the skillet and sauté until translucent.
Stir in the spices and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the chicken to the skillet, pour in the broth and nuka-pickle brine, ensuring the legs are partially submerged.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cover loosely with foil, and cook for 25 minutes until the chicken reaches 165°F.
Uncover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until the sauce thickens to a glaze, about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle with fresh cilantro, adjust seasoning, and serve hot over cauliflower rice or steamed veggies.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
